# AI Act Navigator

> The free, plain-English hub for the EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689). Independent guidance, free tools and trustworthy updates for any company that builds or uses AI.

AI Act Navigator explains the EU AI Act in plain English and gives away the tools to act on it: a risk-tier classifier, a provider-vs-deployer obligations checker, a high-risk (Annex III) lookup, a glossary, a living deadline tracker, and downloadable PDF guides. Independent (no software to sell), always-updated, and sourced to primary references (EUR-Lex, the European Commission, the EU AI Office). This is guidance, not legal advice. Not affiliated with the EU.

Key facts the site is built on: the AI Act entered into force on 1 August 2024; prohibited practices (Art. 5) and the AI literacy duty (Art. 4) apply from 2 February 2025; GPAI, governance and penalties from 2 August 2025; most high-risk (Annex III) and transparency obligations from 2 August 2026; Annex I product rules from 2 August 2027. A proposed "Digital Omnibus" would postpone the Annex III high-risk date to 2 December 2027, but as of June 2026 it is not yet law.

## Core guides
- [What is the EU AI Act? — the complete guide](https://aiact-navigator.com/ai-act): Pillar explainer — risk tiers, who must comply, extraterritorial reach, GPAI, obligations, deadlines, penalties.
- [EU AI Act timeline & deadlines](https://aiact-navigator.com/timeline): Living tracker of the phased application dates and the proposed Digital Omnibus changes.
- [EU AI Act glossary](https://aiact-navigator.com/glossary): Plain-English definitions of the Act's key terms.

## Free tools
- [AI Act Risk-Tier Classifier](https://aiact-navigator.com/risk-classifier): Answer a few questions to find your AI system's risk tier (prohibited / high-risk / limited / minimal) and your role.
- [Provider vs Deployer Obligations Checker](https://aiact-navigator.com/obligations): Your tailored obligations by role and risk tier, with article references.
- [High-Risk AI Use Cases (Annex III)](https://aiact-navigator.com/high-risk): Searchable lookup of the high-risk areas and regulated-product categories.
- [AI System Inventory](https://aiact-navigator.com/ai-inventory): A guide plus a ready-to-fill CSV template to register every AI system you build or use.
- [AI Act Readiness Checklist (PDF)](https://aiact-navigator.com/checklist): The full readiness journey for providers, deployers and SMEs.

## Topics & guides
- [Prohibited AI practices (Article 5)](https://aiact-navigator.com/topics/prohibited-ai-practices)
- [High-risk AI systems (Annex I & III)](https://aiact-navigator.com/topics/high-risk-ai-systems)
- [General-purpose AI (GPAI) models](https://aiact-navigator.com/topics/general-purpose-ai)
- [Provider vs deployer roles](https://aiact-navigator.com/topics/provider-vs-deployer)
- [The EU AI Act for US & non-EU companies](https://aiact-navigator.com/topics/ai-act-for-us-companies)
- [EU AI Act penalties and fines](https://aiact-navigator.com/topics/ai-act-penalties)
- [EU AI Act vs GDPR](https://aiact-navigator.com/topics/ai-act-vs-gdpr)
- [Transparency obligations (Article 50)](https://aiact-navigator.com/topics/transparency-obligations)

## Governance
- [AI governance for the EU AI Act](https://aiact-navigator.com/ai-governance): Frameworks, tooling and how governance maps to AI Act obligations.
- [ISO/IEC 42001 and the EU AI Act](https://aiact-navigator.com/iso-42001): What the standard covers — and why it is not, on its own, a presumption of conformity.
- [AI literacy training (Article 4)](https://aiact-navigator.com/ai-literacy): The in-force duty, who it applies to, and how to build a programme.

## About
- [About & methodology](https://aiact-navigator.com/about): Who we are, how we keep this accurate, and how we flag unsettled rules.
- [The AI Act Brief newsletter](https://aiact-navigator.com/subscribe): Free plain-English updates as the rules move.
